yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions#

See https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/stable/torch.compiler_ir.html for the full list of aten functions.

class yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.Reduction(*values)[source]#
yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_FunctionCtx(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], *args, **kwargs)[source]#

not implemented

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten___and___Scalar(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = '__and___Scalar') → str[source]#

inplace and, we assume inplace modifications were removed

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten___and___Tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = '__and___Tensor') → str[source]#

inplace and, we assume inplace modifications were removed

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten___lshift___Scalar(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = '__lshift___Scalar') → str[source]#

left shift

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten___lshift___Tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = '__lshift___Tensor') → str[source]#

left shift

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten___or___Scalar(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = '__or___Scalar') → str[source]#

inplace or, we assume inplace modifications were removed

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten___or___Tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = '__or___Tensor') → str[source]#

inplace or, we assume inplace modifications were removed

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_addr(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, vec1: str, vec2: str, beta: float = 1.0, alpha: float = 1.0, name: str = 'addr') → str[source]#

Computes the outer product of vec1 and vec2 and adds it to the matrix x.

Implements out = beta * x + alpha * outer(vec1, vec2).

Computes element-wise two-argument arctangent atan2(y, x).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_atanh(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str) → str[source]#

atanh

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_atleast_1d(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'atleast_1d') → str[source]#

atleast_1d — Ensures the result has at least 1 dimension.

A 0-D (scalar) tensor is unsqueezed to shape (1,). Tensors with rank >= 1 are returned unchanged.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_atleast_2d(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'atleast_2d') → str[source]#

atleast_2d — Ensures the result has at least 2 dimensions.

  • rank 0 → shape (1, 1)

  • rank 1 → shape (1, n)

  • rank >= 2 → unchanged

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_atleast_3d(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'atleast_3d') → str[source]#

atleast_3d — Ensures the result has at least 3 dimensions.

  • rank 0 → shape (1, 1, 1)

  • rank 1 → shape (1, n, 1)

  • rank 2 → shape (m, n, 1)

  • rank >= 3 → unchanged

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_auto_functionalized(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], wrapped_func, *args: Sequence[str], **kwargs) → str[source]#

identity, calling a local function

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_batch_norm(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, weight: str | None = None, bias: str | None = None, running_mean: str | None = None, running_var: str | None = None, training: bool = False, momentum: float = 0.9, eps: float = 1e-05, cudnn_enabled: bool = False, name: str = 'batch_norm') → str[source]#

batch normalization

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_bilinear(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], input1: str, input2: str, weight: str, bias: str | None = None, name: str = 'bilinear') → str[source]#

Computes the bilinear form.

Computes output[..., k] = Σ_{i,j} input1[..., i] * weight[k, i, j] * input2[..., j].

Parameters:

  • g – graph builder

  • sts – optional shape/type information

  • outputs – output names

  • input1 – first input tensor of shape (…, H1)

  • input2 – second input tensor of shape (…, H2)

  • weight – weight tensor of shape (out, H1, H2)

  • bias – optional bias tensor of shape (out,)

  • name – node name prefix

Returns:

Returns the output tensor of shape (…, out).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_binary_cross_entropy(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, target: str, weight: str | None = None, reduction: int = 1, size_average: bool | None = None, reduce: bool | None = None, name: str = 'binary_cross_entropy') → str[source]#

binary_cross_entropy

Computes -[target * log(x) + (1 - target) * log(1 - x)] then applies weight and reduction.

size_average and reduce are deprecated legacy arguments. When present they are mapped to the reduction enum value following PyTorch semantics.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_binary_cross_entropy_with_logits(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, target: str, weight: str | None = None, pos_weight: str | None = None, reduction: int = 1, size_average: bool | None = None, reduce: bool | None = None, name: str = 'binary_cross_entropy_with_logits') → str[source]#

binary_cross_entropy_with_logits

Numerically stable formula: max(x, 0) - x * target + log(1 + exp(-abs(x))). When pos_weight is given, the positive term is scaled.

size_average and reduce are deprecated legacy arguments. When present they are mapped to the reduction enum value following PyTorch semantics.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_bincount(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, weights: str | None = None, minlength: int = 0, name: str = 'bincount') → str[source]#

Converts aten.bincount to ONNX.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_bitwise_xor__Tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = 'bitwise_xor__Tensor') → str[source]#

bitwise xor

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_block_diag(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], *tensors: str) → str[source]#

Creates a block diagonal matrix from the provided tensors.

Each input is normalised to 2-D (0-D scalars become [1, 1], 1-D vectors of length n become [1, n]) and then placed on the diagonal of the output 2-D matrix.

The function handles two FX graph representations of aten::block_diag:

  • torch.export.export lowers to aten.block_diag.default whose schema is Tensor[] tensors, so the interpreter passes a single list argument; in that case tensors is a 1-tuple containing the list.

  • The CustomTracer path keeps the Python-level torch.block_diag(x, t1, t2, ...) call intact, so each tensor name arrives as a separate positional argument.

Both forms are normalised to a flat sequence of tensor names.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_bmm(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str) → str[source]#

Computes the Cartesian product of input 1D tensors.

Returns a 1D tensor when a single input is given, or a 2D tensor where each row contains one combination of elements from the input tensors.

cross_entropy — wrapper for torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy.

Handles the deprecated size_average and reduce kwargs and delegates to aten_cross_entropy_loss().

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_cudnn_batch_norm(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, weight: str, bias: str | None, running_mean: str | None, running_var: str | None, training: bool = False, momentum: float = 0.9, eps: float = 1e-05, name: str = 'cudnn_batch_norm') → Tuple[str, str, str][source]#

cudnn_batch_norm

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_cumprod(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, dim: int, dtype: torch.dtype | None = None, name: str = 'cumprod') → str[source]#

cumprod

ONNX has no native CumProd op. For floating-point dtypes we lower to Exp(CumSum(Log(|x|))) * sign where the cumulative sign is computed in O(n) memory via the parity of the running count of negative elements. For integer/bool dtypes we fall back to a lower-triangular masked ReduceProd(): vals[..., i, j] = x[..., j] if j <= i and 1 otherwise, then ReduceProd along j.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_cumsum(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, dim: str, dtype: torch.dtype | None = None, name: str = 'cumsum') → str[source]#

cumsum

embedding

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_erfc(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'erfc') → str[source]#

Computes the complementary error function, erfc(x) = 1 - erf(x).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_erfinv(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'erfinv') → str[source]#

Computes the inverse error function via Winitzki approximation + Newton refinement.

exp2 — Computes 2 ** x, casting integer inputs to float32 first.

fft_fft

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_fft_fft2(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, s: Tuple[int, int] | None = None, dim: Tuple[int, int] = -1, norm: str | None = None, name: str = 'fft_fft') → str[source]#

fft_fft2

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_fft_fftshift(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, dim: int | List[int] | None = None, name: str = 'fft_fftshift') → str[source]#

Shifts the zero-frequency component to the center of the spectrum.

Equivalent to rolling each specified dimension by n // 2 where n is the size of that dimension. Supports dynamic shapes.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_fft_ifft(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, n: int | None = None, dim: int = -1, norm: str | None = None, name: str = 'fft_ifft') → str[source]#

fft_ifft

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_fft_ifft2(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, s: Tuple[int, int] | None = None, dim: Tuple[int, int] = -1, norm: str | None = None, name: str = 'fft_ifft2') → str[source]#

fft_fft2

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_fft_ifftshift(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, dim: int | List[int] | None = None, name: str = 'fft_ifftshift') → str[source]#

Computes the inverse of fftshift.

Equivalent to rolling each specified dimension by -(n // 2) (i.e. rolling by +n // 2 in the opposite direction), where n is the size of that dimension. Supports dynamic shapes.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_fill_Scalar(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, v: str, name: str = 'fill_Scalar') → str[source]#

Implements fmod for both scalar and tensor second arguments.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_fmod_Scalar(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, scalar: float, name='fmod_Scalar') → str[source]#

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_fmod_Tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = 'fmod_Tensor') → str[source]#

Implements fmod.Tensor.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_frac(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'frac') → str[source]#

Computes the fractional part of each element: frac(x) = x - trunc(x).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_frexp(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'frexp') → Tuple[str, str][source]#

Delegates torch.frexp to aten_frexp_Tensor().

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_frexp_Tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'frexp_Tensor') → Tuple[str, str][source]#

Decomposes x into mantissa and exponent so that x = mantissa * 2 ** exponent with 0.5 <= |mantissa| < 1.

Returns:

A tuple (mantissa, exponent) where mantissa has the same dtype as x and exponent is an INT32 tensor with the same shape.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_full(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], size: str, fill_value: float | None, dtype: torch.dtype | None = None, layout=None, device: torch.device | None = None, pin_memory=None, requires_grad: bool = False, name: str = 'full') → str[source]#

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_ge_Tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str) → str[source]#

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_gelu(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, approximate: str = 'none', name: str = 'gelu') → str[source]#

gelu

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_geqrf(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'geqrf') → Tuple[str, str][source]#

Computes the LAPACK-style packed QR factorization via Householder reflectors.

Returns (a, tau) where a (shape (m, n)) holds the upper-triangular R factor in its upper triangle and the normalised Householder vectors in its strictly lower triangle, and tau (shape (min(m, n),)) holds the corresponding scalar factors.

The input must have a statically-known 2-D shape because the Householder loop is unrolled at graph-construction time.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_getattr(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, attr_name: str, name: str = 'getattr') → str[source]#

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_lift_fresh_copy(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str) → str[source]#

identity

Computes the cross product of two 3-element vectors along dim.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_linalg_det(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'linalg_det') → str[source]#

Computes the determinant of a square matrix or batch of square matrices.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_linalg_slogdet(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'linalg_slogdet') → Tuple[str, str][source]#

Computes the sign and the natural log of the absolute value of the determinant.

Computes the dot product of two batches of vectors along dim.

reduce *

linear

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_log(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'log') → str[source]#

log

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_log10(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'log10') → str[source]#

log10 — Computes log base 10 as log(x) * log10(e).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_log1p(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'log1p') → str[source]#

log1p — Computes log(1 + x).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_log2(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'log2') → str[source]#

log2 — Computes log base 2 as log(x) * log2(e).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_log_sigmoid(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'log_sigmoid') → str[source]#

log_sigmoid

Computes log(sigmoid(x)) = log(1 / (1 + exp(-x))), which equals -softplus(-x).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_log_sigmoid_forward(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'log_sigmoid_forward') → Tuple[str, str][source]#

log_sigmoid_forward — returns (log_sigmoid(x), buffer).

The second return value (buffer) is an implementation detail used by the backward pass (it stores min(x, 0)). During forward-only export we return a scalar zero placeholder for it.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_log_softmax_int(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, dim: int = -1, unnamed: bool = False, dtype: torch.dtype | None = None, name: str = 'log_softmax_int') → str[source]#

logsoftmax

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_logaddexp(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = 'logaddexp') → str[source]#

logaddexp — Computes log(exp(x) + exp(y)) in a numerically stable way.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_logaddexp2(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = 'logaddexp2') → str[source]#

logaddexp2 — Computes log2(2**x + 2**y) in a numerically stable way.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_logical_and(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name='and') → str[source]#

maximum along a dimension, returning (values, indices).

When both outputs are requested this lowers to a single TopK (k=1) rather than a ReduceMax + ArgMax pair.

minimum along a dimension, returning (values, indices).

Same lowering strategy as aten_max_dim(): a single TopK op (with largest=0) when both outputs are needed.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_min_other(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, y: str, name: str = 'min_other') → str[source]#

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_mish(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, inplace: bool = False, name: str = 'mish') → str[source]#

mish

Computes x * tanh(softplus(x)) = x * tanh(log(1 + exp(x))). Uses the ONNX Mish operator (opset ≥ 18).

norm.ScalarOpt_dim — computes the p-norm along dim.

This is a general vector norm along specified dimensions, equivalent to torch.norm(x, p=p, dim=dim, keepdim=keepdim).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_pairwise_distance(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x1: str, x2: str, p: float = 2.0, eps: float = 1e-06, keepdim: bool = False, name: str = 'pairwise_distance') → str[source]#

pairwise_distance

Computes ||x1 - x2 + eps||_p along the last dimension. Integer inputs are promoted to float32 (matching PyTorch behaviour).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_repeat(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, repeats: str, name: str = 'repeat') → str[source]#

repeat

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_repeat_interleave(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, repeats: str | List[int] | None = None, dim: int | None = None, output_size: Tuple[int, ...] | None = None, name: str = 'repeat_interleave') → str[source]#

repeat_interleave

if repeats is an integer, then repeat_interleave is just a repeat in the other dimension and a reshape.

<<<

import torch

x = torch.tensor([[0, 1, 2], [3, 4, 5]])
x.repeat_interleave(2, dim=0)

>>>

is equivalent to:

<<<

import torch

x = torch.tensor([[0, 1, 2], [3, 4, 5]])
x.repeat((1, 2)).reshape((-1, x.shape[1]))

>>>

When repeats is a tensor, each line is multiplied by a different number and this strategy no longer works. There are multiple strategies. Here is one.

<<<

import torch

x = torch.tensor([[0, 1, 2], [3, 4, 5]])
times = torch.tensor([2, 3], dtype=torch.int64)
y = x.repeat_interleave(times, dim=0)
print("repeat_interleave")
print(y)

ci = times.cumsum(dim=0)
rows = torch.arange(ci[-1], dtype=torch.int64) < ci.reshape((-1, 1))
srows = times.shape[0] - rows.to(torch.int64).sum(axis=0)
indices = srows.reshape((-1,))
print("decomposed")
print(x[indices, :])

>>>

    repeat_interleave
    tensor([[0, 1, 2],
            [0, 1, 2],
            [3, 4, 5],
            [3, 4, 5],
            [3, 4, 5]])
    decomposed
    tensor([[0, 1, 2],
            [0, 1, 2],
            [3, 4, 5],
            [3, 4, 5],
            [3, 4, 5]])
yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_repeat_interleave_Tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, repeats: str | None = None, dim: int | None = None, output_size: Tuple[int, ...] | None = None, name: str = 'repeat_interleave_Tensor') → str[source]#

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_sigmoid_backward(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], out_grad: str, y: str) → str[source]#

sigmoid backward

See pytorch/pytorch. conj_physical = identity for real number.

return out_grad * (y * (1 - y)).conj_physical()
yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_sign(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'sign') → str[source]#

sign

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_signbit(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, name: str = 'signbit') → str[source]#

Returns a boolean tensor indicating where the input has a negative sign bit.

For floating-point types this includes negative zero (-0.0), which has its IEEE 754 sign bit set even though -0.0 < 0 is False. The implementation reinterprets the float bits as an unsigned integer with BitCast (requires opset >= 21) and extracts the sign bit with BitwiseAnd. For integer types a simple x < 0 comparison suffices.

Returns:

A boolean tensor with the same shape as x; True where the sign bit is set (i.e. the element is negative or negative zero).

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_silu(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, inplace: bool = False, name: str = 'silu') → str[source]#

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_simple_loop_for(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], n_iter: str, body_fn: str, operands: Tuple[str, ...], concatenation_dims: Sequence[str], name='simple_for_loop') → str | Tuple[str, ...][source]#

Converts a custom op higher_ops::loop_for_onnx... into e sequence of node.

Parameters:

  • g – GreaphBuilder

  • sts – if not defined, torch does not know the output shapes

  • outputs – output names

  • args – input argument known at export time

  • body – GraphProto, the loop body

  • reduction_dim – the dimension to follow when aggregating the list of tensors after the loop ran

  • name – to give the onnx nodes a name

Returns:

output names

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_smooth_l1_loss(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, target: str, reduction: int = 1, beta: float = 1.0, size_average: bool | None = None, reduce: bool | None = None, name: str = 'smooth_l1_loss') → str[source]#

smooth_l1_loss (Huber loss)

For each element z = |x - target|:

  • 0.5 * z^2 / beta when z < beta

  • z - 0.5 * beta otherwise

size_average and reduce are deprecated legacy arguments. When present they are mapped to the reduction enum value following PyTorch semantics.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_soft_margin_loss(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, target: str, reduction: int = 1, size_average: bool | None = None, reduce: bool | None = None, name: str = 'soft_margin_loss') → str[source]#

soft_margin_loss

Computes log(1 + exp(-x * target)) then applies reduction.

size_average and reduce are deprecated legacy arguments. When present they are mapped to the reduction enum value following PyTorch semantics.

The stable flag is accepted for API compatibility but ignored.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_tensor(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, indices: Tuple[Any, ...] | None = None, dtype: Any | None = None, device: Any | None = None, requires_grad: bool = False) → str[source]#

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_tensor_split(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, indices_or_sections: int | List[int] | str, dim: int = 0, name: str = 'tensor_split') → Tuple[str, ...][source]#

Dispatches tensor_split to the sections or indices variant.

Handles the tracing path where indices_or_sections may be a graph variable name (str) referring to a constant integer tensor.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_tensor_split_Tensor_sections(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, sections: int, dim: int = 0, name: str = 'tensor_split_Tensor_sections') → Tuple[str, ...][source]#

Handles the aten::tensor_split.Tensor_sections overload.

Delegates to aten_tensor_split_sections().

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_tensor_split_indices(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, indices: List[int], dim: int = 0, name: str = 'tensor_split_indices') → Tuple[str, ...][source]#

Splits x at the given index positions along dim.

Unlike split sizes, indices are absolute positions in the tensor, not lengths. Out-of-range indices are clamped to [0, size].

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_tensor_split_sections(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, sections: int, dim: int = 0, name: str = 'tensor_split_sections') → Tuple[str, ...][source]#

Splits x into sections chunks along dim.

Unlike torch.split(), the chunk sizes may differ by at most one element so that exactly sections chunks are produced.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.aten_tensor_split_tensor_indices_or_sections(g: GraphBuilder, sts: Dict[str, Any] | None, outputs: List[str], x: str, indices_or_sections: str, dim: int = 0, name: str = 'tensor_split_Tensor_indices_or_sections') → Tuple[str, ...][source]#

Handles the aten::tensor_split.Tensor_indices_or_sections overload.

Delegates to aten_tensor_split().

where

This function may introduce some type issues when ‘x’ and ‘other’ are both floats. Implicit cast may be done by torch. Checks what happens after this node.

xlogy: computes x * log(y) with the convention that xlogy(0, y) == 0.

yobx.torch.interpreter._aten_functions.prepare_inputs_homogeneous_operator(g: GraphBuilder, *args: Sequence[str], f: Callable | None = None, outputs: List[str] | None = None, name: str | None = None, sts: Any | None = None, check_shape: bool = True, op_type: str | None = None, use_left: bool = False, force_type: int | None = None) → Tuple[str, ...][source]#

Casts any inputs to ensure all inputs share the same type.

op_type can be specified to bypass some cases with ambiguities such as a float multiplied with an integer.

Parameters:

  • g – GraphBuilder

  • args – operator arguments

  • f – function calling the operator

  • outputs – output names

  • sts – known shapes and types

  • check_shape – extra verification for shapes

  • op_type – operator type (onnx name)

  • use_left – if the operator is in one inplaced modification then the type of the left side

  • force_type – if not None, choose this type and cast the inputs

Returns:

new inputs

previous
